tourism_level.crowds: Peak during Fêtes de Genève weekend; otherwise moderate as locals leave on holiday and business travel pauses.
tourism_level.pricing: Soft mid-week; spikes around Fêtes de Genève weekend.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Festive, slightly emptier on weekdays, lake-focused throughout.

events_holidays.name: Swiss National Day (Fête Nationale)
events_holidays.description: Public holiday with parades, alpenhorn concerts, brunches on hill farms, and fireworks at lakefront villages.
events_holidays.notes: Most shops closed; restaurants busy. Trains and lake boats run holiday schedules.

events_holidays.name: Fêtes de Genève
events_holidays.description: Geneva's signature summer festival — food village, concerts, and the headline fireworks display over the harbour seen by hundreds of thousands.
events_holidays.notes: Book restaurants weeks ahead. Lakefront pedestrianised; tram diversions.

events_holidays.name: Assumption Day
events_holidays.description: Not a public holiday in Geneva but observed in some neighbouring French border areas; minor cross-border disruption.
events_holidays.notes: No impact within the city.

unique_experiences.where: Lakefront — both shores, Quai du Mont-Blanc and Quai Gustave-Ador
unique_experiences.description: The signature pyrotechnic display: 60+ minutes choreographed to music, fired from barges in the harbour.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Arrive 2–3 hours early for prime spots. Bains des Pâquis pier is excellent if reservation-free standing room remains.

unique_experiences.where: Hill farms near Geneva (e.g., Mont Salève farms, Jura alpages)
unique_experiences.description: Switzerland's farms open their doors for a traditional brunch — cheese, rösti, charcuterie, fresh bread.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Reserve weeks in advance via Schweizer Bauern. Bring cash.

unique_experiences.description: Watch the fireworks from the lake itself, on a CGN paddle-steamer or modern boat with dinner.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Books out months in advance. Confirm boarding times.

unique_experiences.description: Geneva's classic open-air pool complex on the lake: Olympic pool, water slides, lake access, restaurants.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Arrive by 10:30 for shade. Day pass tickets at the gate.

weather.precipitation: Heat eases gradually; thunderstorms break the patterns.
weather.general_tips: Best mountain-clarity windows arrive late August. Mornings cool down faster — wonderful for runs and swims.

tourism_level.crowds: Calmer after Fêtes de Genève. End-of-month rentrée brings business and conference travel back.
tourism_level.pricing: Soft mid-week; weekend rates re-firming late month.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Lazy late summer, with peaks of energy on warm weekends.

events_holidays.name: Lake Parade
events_holidays.description: Electronic music parade along the lakefront, drawing tens of thousands.
events_holidays.notes: Check programme; large temporary stages and food vendors line the quais.

events_holidays.name: Late-Summer Vintage Begins
events_holidays.description: First Genevan grape harvest typically begins late month, with early-vintage tasting events at Mandement and Lavaux vineyards.
events_holidays.notes: Some cellar visits programmed; check Geneva Tourism.

unique_experiences.description: Late August evenings bring some of the clearest air of the year — ride up at 19:00 with a picnic for sunset over the entire Léman basin.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Bring passport. Check last cable car down time.

unique_experiences.description: If running, the parade brings stages and trucks along the quais — an end-of-summer street-festival atmosphere.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Check official programme. Tram detours likely.

unique_experiences.activity: Boat to Lausanne for the Day
unique_experiences.description: Long, scenic CGN cruise along the south shore to Lausanne — picnic, museum (Art Brut, Photo Elysée), and ride back at sunset.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Covered by Swiss Travel Pass. Bring a hat for top-deck sun.

unique_experiences.description: After much of Geneva returns from holiday, Carouge's terraces fill with end-of-summer apéro energy.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Tram 12. Reserve dinner at popular bistros.

reasons_to_visit.details: On the first weekend, 50,000+ runners (and many costumed walkers) take over the Old Town for Geneva's signature race — one of the largest urban races in Europe and a celebration of civic identity.

Around December 11–12, Geneva marks the failed 1602 Savoyard siege with torchlit processions through the Old Town in period dress, the breaking of chocolate cauldrons, and the recitation of 'Cé qu'è lainô' across schools and squares.
Jardin Anglais, Noël au Quai, Fusterie, and the Carouge market form a self-contained festive circuit with vin chaud, fondue chalets, and ice rinks.
Light installations across the city extend into mid-December, making after-dark walks especially atmospheric.

reasons_to_visit.reason: Ice Skating at Parc des Bastions
reasons_to_visit.details: A free outdoor ice rink runs in the historic park beneath the Old Town, with cocoa stalls and live music on weekends.
Major resorts (Chamonix, Verbier, Les Portes du Soleil) at full operation. Day trips from Geneva straightforward.

reasons_to_reconsider.details: Persistent fog and overcast skies; bise wind can make outdoor time uncomfortable.
Sunset by 16:45. Plan around the dark and embrace evening illuminations.
December 24 afternoon onward, 25, and 26 see widespread shop, museum, and restaurant closures. Public transport reduced.
Escalade weekend, mid-month conferences, and New Year's Eve all drive sharp rate spikes. Mid-December weekdays are the value sweet spot.
Boats minimal; outdoor cafés closed except heated terraces.
Cold weather suspends the fountain throughout the month.

weather.precipitation: Cold and damp. Frequent fog; snow possible at city level but unusual; consistent above 800m.
weather.general_tips: Pack winter coat, gloves, hat. Grippy shoes for damp cobblestones.

tourism_level.crowds: Escalade weekend extremely busy in the Old Town; otherwise comfortable.
tourism_level.pricing: Escalade weekend spikes; otherwise mid-range with attractive mid-week deals.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Civic-festive: torchlit processions, market stalls, chocolate cauldrons, marathon energy. Geneva at its most distinctive.

events_holidays.name: Course de l'Escalade
events_holidays.description: Massive urban race weekend through the Old Town — competitive, fun-run, and costumed Marmite categories.
events_holidays.notes: Old Town traffic closures from Saturday morning. Even non-runners should visit for the atmosphere.

events_holidays.name: Fête de l'Escalade
events_holidays.description: Geneva's most loved local festival: torchlit processions in 17th-century costume through the Old Town, breaking of chocolate marmite cauldrons full of marzipan vegetables, and recitation of 'Cé qu'è lainô' (the city anthem).
events_holidays.notes: Procession routes published in advance. Wear warm clothes for outdoor torchlight evenings.

events_holidays.name: Christmas Markets Run
events_holidays.description: Jardin Anglais, Noël au Quai, Fusterie, Carouge — all open daily through December 24.
events_holidays.notes: Weekday afternoons quietest.

unique_experiences.activity: Course de l'Escalade Run or Spectate
unique_experiences.description: Run the 4–8 km route through cobbled streets and Reformation-era walls, or watch from Place du Bourg-de-Four with a vin chaud.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Race registration opens months ahead and fills fast.

unique_experiences.where: Old Town — Cour Saint-Pierre to Place du Bourg-de-Four
unique_experiences.description: Hundreds of locals in 17th-century costume process by torchlight, halting at squares to perform Cé qu'è lainô and serve hot vegetable soup.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Free. Arrive early at Place du Bourg-de-Four for the soup. Wear gloves.

unique_experiences.where: Chocolatiers across the city (Auer, Du Rhône, Stettler, Favarger)
unique_experiences.description: Chocolate cauldrons filled with marzipan vegetables, broken with the family recitation 'Ainsi périssent les ennemis de la République!'
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Multiple sizes; whole marmites cost meaningfully more than tasting portions.

unique_experiences.description: Free outdoor rink with rentals available; surrounded by Christmas chalets and the Reformation Wall.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Skate rental fee small; bring gloves.

weather.precipitation: Cold and damp. Snow possible at city level; common in the mountains.
weather.general_tips: Pack for cold; check holiday opening hours carefully.

tourism_level.crowds: Quieter mid-month, then climbing into Christmas week and peaking on New Year's Eve.
tourism_level.pricing: Christmas week and NYE at year-high; mid-December weekdays the value window.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Festive close to the year — markets glowing, restaurants full of long lunches, lake walks under early winter dusk.

events_holidays.name: Christmas Eve & Christmas Day
events_holidays.description: Public holiday — most shops, museums, and many restaurants closed. Lake cruises minimal; trams reduced.
events_holidays.notes: Plan hotel dining or pre-book restaurants known to open.

events_holidays.name: Boxing Day (Saint-Étienne)
events_holidays.description: Public holiday — widespread closures continue.
events_holidays.notes: Some museums open; check websites.

events_holidays.name: New Year's Eve (Saint-Sylvestre)
events_holidays.description: Lakefront fireworks (when scheduled), hotel galas, restaurant prix-fixe menus, and packed late-night bars and clubs.
events_holidays.notes: Restaurant menus often expensive and fixed. Reserve far in advance. Late-night trams operate.

unique_experiences.where: Jardin Anglais → Noël au Quai → Fusterie
unique_experiences.description: Walk between Geneva's main Christmas markets along the lakefront and into the Old Town for chalets, vin chaud, and crafts.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Cash useful; evening fairy lights best.

unique_experiences.activity: Christmas Eve Lake Walk + Hotel Dinner
unique_experiences.where: Quai du Mont-Blanc + a grand hotel restaurant
unique_experiences.description: Pre-dinner lakefront stroll under festive lighting, followed by a celebratory dinner at Le Chat-Botté, Bayview, or Il Lago.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Book months ahead. Many menus are fixed.

unique_experiences.description: Reliable snow, full lift operations, and a 90-minute trip from Geneva.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Book transfers early during holiday week.

unique_experiences.where: Quai du Général-Guisan or Quai du Mont-Blanc
unique_experiences.description: Hotel galas, dinner cruises, and (some years) public fireworks over the lake.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Confirm whether public fireworks scheduled. Restaurant reservations long-since locked in.

reasons_to_visit.details: Antigel runs through most of February with its strongest weekend programme — experimental concerts, dance, and cross-disciplinary performance scattered across Geneva and its suburbs, drawing creative crowds without overwhelming the city.
Snow cover in nearby Chamonix, Megève, Verbier, Les Portes du Soleil, and Crans-Montana is at its best, and Geneva is the easiest big-city base in Europe for a single-day ski sortie.
Outside ski-week half-terms, Geneva itself remains uncrowded, with empty museums and easy bookings at top restaurants like Le Chat-Botté and Domaine de Châteauvieux.
By month-end sunset has crept past 18:00, giving an extra useful hour to walk the lakefront or catch the late light on Mont Blanc.

reasons_to_visit.reason: Vacherin Mont d'Or & Late Fondue Season
reasons_to_visit.details: February is the last reliable month for vacherin Mont d'Or (the unctuous oven-baked Swiss cheese specialty) before it disappears in spring.
Geneva's international community hosts Lunar New Year dinners, lion-dance performances, and pop-up markets — particularly at venues around the UN quarter and Plainpalais.

reasons_to_reconsider.details: Early February often delivers Geneva's lowest temperatures. The bise can be brutal and the lake basin retains cold longer than the surrounding hills.
French, Swiss, and UK half-term weeks fall in February. Ski resorts get crowded, the airport queues lengthen, and lift prices peak — even if the city itself stays calm.
Cold and wind continue to suspend the fountain regularly. Don't budget time around seeing it spray.
CGN's winter schedule continues, with most boats running only short shuttle services. Full lake tours don't return until spring.
When the bise breaks and milder Atlantic air arrives, Geneva can sit under unbroken low cloud for days — head up to Salève or Jura for blue sky above the inversion.
Terraces remain shuttered or heated only sporadically. Plan around interior restaurants and cafés.

weather.precipitation: Cold and dry under the bise, alternating with milder, wetter Atlantic systems. Snow possible at city level but unusual; reliable cover above 1,200m.
weather.general_tips: Full winter kit. Use clear bise days for views, grey days for museums and Antigel events.

tourism_level.crowds: Quiet, with mid-week being the quietest. Ski-week half-terms can push up airport queues and ski-resort prices.
tourism_level.pricing: Hotel rates climb slightly from January lows around French and Swiss half-terms but remain among the year's cheapest.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Late winter quiet animated by Antigel and Valentine's Day dining.

events_holidays.name: Antigel Festival (peak weekends)
events_holidays.description: Headliner concerts and signature dance/performance pieces typically fall in the first two weekends of February.
events_holidays.notes: Buy tickets in advance. Many shows include a TPG-organised shuttle from Cornavin.

events_holidays.name: Lunar New Year Events
events_holidays.description: Asian cultural associations and consulates host concerts, dinners, and lion-dance performances around Plainpalais and the UN quarter.
events_holidays.notes: Check the city's agenda culturel — most events are free or low-cost.

events_holidays.name: Valentine's Day
events_holidays.description: Geneva's fine-dining restaurants run prix-fixe menus — Le Chat-Botté, Tsé Fung, Domaine de Châteauvieux, and Bayview all book up weeks ahead.
events_holidays.notes: Reserve a month in advance. Lake-view tables go first.

unique_experiences.activity: Antigel Performance in a Suburban Venue
unique_experiences.description: Catch a contemporary dance piece in a town hall theatre, an experimental DJ set in a sports centre, or a brass band reworking pop hits in a bus depot.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Programme is bilingual French/English. Allow 60–90 minutes by tram/bus to reach outlying venues.

unique_experiences.activity: Ski Half-Day at Les Portes du Soleil or Megève
unique_experiences.description: A Geneva-based half-day skier can drive to Les Gets or Megève, ski from late morning to dusk, and be back in the city for dinner.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: AlpyBus and Mountain Drop-Offs do door-to-door from Geneva hotels. Buy lift passes online to skip queues.

unique_experiences.where: Musée d'Art et d'Histoire, Rue Charles-Galland
unique_experiences.description: Spend a quiet morning with Geneva's home-town masterpieces: Witz's 1444 'Miraculous Draught of Fishes' (the first identifiable landscape in Western art, painted from the Geneva lakefront) and the Reformation-era collection.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Closed Mondays. Free permanent collection. Bring binoculars to study altarpiece details.

unique_experiences.where: Bistros in Eaux-Vives, Carouge, and Pâquis (e.g., Café du Soleil, Les Armures)
unique_experiences.description: Order a whole vacherin baked in its spruce box, dipped with steamed potatoes and charcuterie — quintessential late-winter Swiss comfort.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Best at restaurants serving AOP Vacherin du Haut-Doubs or Vacherin Mont d'Or Suisse. Pair with a local Chasselas.

weather.precipitation: Slowly warming. First snowdrops appear in Parc des Bastions. Bise still possible but milder spells become more common.
weather.general_tips: Daylight extends meaningfully. Excellent time for an afternoon Mont Salève cable car ride to escape city inversions.

tourism_level.crowds: Quiet except during Genevan, French, or Italian half-term weeks. Mountain resorts crowded; the city itself comfortable.
tourism_level.pricing: Still soft, with weekend rates ticking up around ski half-term traffic.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: End-of-winter calm with the first hints of pre-spring energy along the lakefront on sunny afternoons.

events_holidays.name: Antigel Closing Weekend
events_holidays.description: The festival's final weekend usually includes a flagship concert and a large-scale public performance piece.
events_holidays.notes: Closing events draw both insiders and curious visitors — book early.

events_holidays.name: Swiss & French School Half-Terms
events_holidays.description: Geneva's local schools take a one-week ski break, as do many French départements (rolling schedule).
events_holidays.notes: Ski day trips will be busier; in the city itself, expect bumped-up family activities at MAH and the Botanical Gardens.

unique_experiences.activity: Mont Salève Cable Car & Plateau Snowshoe
unique_experiences.where: Téléphérique du Salève, Étrembières (France) — Bus 8 from Geneva
unique_experiences.description: Ride the cable car above the often-fogged-in basin to the Salève plateau, with panoramic views of Geneva, Lake Geneva, the Jura, and the entire Mont Blanc massif.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Bring passport (France). Snowshoes rent at the top in winter. Allow half a day. The plateau restaurant La Corraterie is a strong lunch stop.

unique_experiences.description: Wander Carouge's pastel King Sardinian streets, browse independent jewellers, milliners, and chocolatiers, and pick up cheese and bread from the Saturday morning market.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Tram 12. Many ateliers open Wednesday–Saturday only.

unique_experiences.where: Avenue de la Paix 17 — Bus 8 or Tram 15
unique_experiences.description: Geneva's most thought-provoking museum walks visitors through the history of humanitarian action via three immersive pavilions: defending dignity, restoring family links, and reducing natural risks.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Allow 2 hours. Combine with a walk past the Palais des Nations and the Broken Chair sculpture.

unique_experiences.activity: Aiguille du Midi Cable Car Day Trip
unique_experiences.description: Late February's longer days and stable high pressure produce some of the year's clearest views from 3,842m above Chamonix.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Book the cable car slot online; the 'Step into the Void' glass box is worth the queue. Bus or train from Geneva (90 minutes).

reasons_to_visit.details: After the first week, January is Geneva's quietest tourist month. Lakefront hotels and even some Right Bank business hotels drop to their lowest rates, and the Old Town's museums (MAH, Patek Philippe, Maison Tavel) feel almost private.

Late January through early February brings the Antigel Festival — three weeks of experimental music, dance, and performance across unusual venues in Geneva and surrounding suburbs, energising an otherwise sleepy month.

January is peak season for cheese fondue (Genevan style with vacherin) and raclette in Old Town caves and Carouge bistros — perfect with a glass of local Chasselas after a cold walk along Quai du Mont-Blanc.
Geneva Airport and Cornavin station put you within 60–90 minutes of Chamonix, Les Portes du Soleil, Verbier, and Megève. January reliably delivers deep snow without the half-term crowds.
Genevan soldes start in early January, with significant discounts in Rue du Rhône boutiques, Globus, Manor, and the watch and chocolate shops that line the right bank.

Cold, dry high-pressure days (bise) clear the lake of fog and produce some of the year's best views of Mont Blanc from Quai Wilson and the Jet d'Eau jetty — the fountain itself usually pauses in deep cold but the panorama makes up for it.

reasons_to_reconsider.reason: Cold, Damp Bise Wind & Lake Fog
reasons_to_reconsider.details: Geneva sits at the bottom of a basin and traps cold air. The bise (northeasterly wind) can make 3°C feel like -5°C, and lake fog can shroud the city for days at a time, killing the views.
Geneva's signature landmark is shut down whenever there is risk of freezing spray (and during heavy bise), which happens frequently in January. Don't plan your trip around it.
With sunrise near 8:15 and sunset around 17:00, sightseeing windows are tight. Museums and indoor itineraries work better than ambitious walking days.
New Year's Day and the Restauration de la République (a Geneva-specific holiday observed informally) leave most shops and many restaurants closed. Public transport runs reduced timetables.
CGN paddle-steamer service is on its sparsest schedule, Bains des Pâquis swimming is for sauna users only, and most lakefront terraces are shut.

reasons_to_reconsider.reason: Higher Risk of Strikes / Disruptions in France
reasons_to_reconsider.details: Cross-border commuters into Geneva are exposed to occasional French rail or transport strikes in January, which can complicate day trips to Annecy, Chamonix, or Lyon.

weather.precipitation: Mix of rain, sleet, and occasional snow in the city — accumulation rare at lake level but common above 800m. Frequent fog and overcast skies, with windows of brilliant sun when the bise blows.
weather.general_tips: Pack warm, waterproof layers, a windproof shell against the bise, and shoes with grip for damp cobblestones. Bring a daylight-priority itinerary.

tourism_level.crowds: Very quiet after January 2. Old Town museums, the Reformation Wall, and the Jet d'Eau jetty are essentially empty. Cornavin station and the airport are calm except for ski-day Saturdays.
tourism_level.pricing: Hotel rates drop sharply after January 2 to the lowest levels of the year, with the Right Bank lakefront and Pâquis offering the steepest discounts.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Quiet post-holiday lull. The city feels like it belongs to residents. Christmas lights typically come down in the first week.

events_holidays.name: New Year's Day (Nouvel An)
events_holidays.description: Public holiday across Switzerland. Almost all shops, museums, and many restaurants are closed. TPG buses and trams run on a Sunday schedule with reductions.
events_holidays.notes: Plan a quiet day with a hotel brunch and a lakeside walk. A handful of hotel restaurants and Pâquis brasseries stay open.

events_holidays.name: Berchtoldstag
events_holidays.description: Observed in most Swiss cantons. Geneva does not officially observe it, but many businesses still close or operate short hours.
events_holidays.notes: Check museum websites before planning indoor visits.

events_holidays.name: Soldes d'Hiver Begin
events_holidays.description: Winter sales kick off in Rue du Rhône, Rue du Marché, and Carouge boutiques with reductions on Swiss watches, leather goods, and luxury fashion.
events_holidays.notes: Best selection in the first week; deeper discounts later in the month.

events_holidays.name: End of Christmas Illuminations
events_holidays.description: Lighting in the Old Town, Rues Basses, and around the Jardin Anglais is removed by January 6–8.
events_holidays.notes: Visit before January 6 if you want the festive evening atmosphere.

unique_experiences.description: Wander the world's most important watchmaking collection in near silence — January afternoons are the closest you'll get to a private tour.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Closed Sundays and Mondays. Allow 2–3 hours. Free audio guide is excellent.

unique_experiences.where: Café des Négociants, Le Pain Quotidien, Le Vieux Carouge
unique_experiences.description: Walk across the Arve to Carouge's grid of pastel facades and try fondue moitié-moitié or fondue with vacherin Mont d'Or, paired with a chilled glass of local Chasselas.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Reserve ahead for weekend evenings. Tram 12 from Bel-Air drops you in the centre of Carouge.

unique_experiences.activity: Musée d'Art et d'Histoire (MAH) Deep Dive
unique_experiences.description: Geneva's flagship museum is free for the permanent collection — wander Roman bronzes, Konrad Witz's Miraculous Draught of Fishes, and Hodler landscapes with almost no other visitors.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Closed Mondays. Combine with a coffee in the basement café, then walk to the Reformation Wall through Parc des Bastions.

unique_experiences.activity: Old Town Walk with a Vin Chaud Stop
unique_experiences.where: Place du Bourg-de-Four, Cathédrale Saint-Pierre, Promenade de la Treille
unique_experiences.description: Climb the cobbled lanes to the cathedral, view the Mont Blanc panorama from the Treille terrace, then duck into a wine bar for a vin chaud or a glass of Genevan Gamay.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Wear grippy shoes for icy paving. The cathedral towers may be closed in the deepest cold — check at the door.
unique_experiences.best_time: Late afternoon to catch sunset over the cathedral

weather.precipitation: Cold spells continue, with the chance of bise-driven sub-zero days alternating with milder, rainier westerly weather. Snow possible but not reliable at city level; abundant in the surrounding Alps.
weather.general_tips: This is the optimal window for ski day trips. Watch SBB and SNCF strike alerts if planning France-side excursions.

tourism_level.crowds: Lowest tourism point of the year apart from early December. Even Antigel events feel local rather than touristy.
tourism_level.pricing: Hotel rates remain at the year's floor. Excellent deals on lakefront suites and serviced apartments in Eaux-Vives.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: A working city in the depth of winter, energised once Antigel begins in late January.

events_holidays.name: Antigel Festival Opens
events_holidays.description: Three-week experimental music, dance, and performance festival across Geneva and surrounding municipalities, often in unusual venues (factories, gyms, public swimming pools).
events_holidays.notes: Check the Antigel programme — many events sell out and require advance booking. Tram and bus connections to suburban venues are well organised.

events_holidays.name: Geneva International Film Festival Tous Écrans (occasional January spill-over)
events_holidays.description: Some retrospective screenings continue into early January in select years.
events_holidays.notes: Check the GIFF website for current programming.

unique_experiences.activity: Ski or Snowshoe Day Trip to Chamonix
unique_experiences.where: Aiguille du Midi, Brévent, Le Tour (Mont Blanc area)
unique_experiences.description: Hop a 90-minute SAT bus or train from Geneva and ski under Mont Blanc, or take the Aiguille du Midi cable car to 3,842m for one of the most dramatic mountain panoramas in the Alps.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Buy combined Mont-Blanc Unlimited or Chamonix Le Pass tickets in advance. Bring sunglasses — UV at altitude is intense even in cloud.

unique_experiences.description: Geneva's beloved lake baths run a winter sauna, hammam, and Turkish bath programme, ending with the boldest plunging into the freezing lake.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Bring sandals, towel, and swimwear. Open into the evening; book massages in advance.

unique_experiences.where: Various venues across Geneva and Grand Genève
unique_experiences.description: Catch an electronic concert in a former industrial hall, an avant-garde dance piece in a community swimming pool, or a midnight DJ set in an unmarked basement.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Programme drops in early January — popular shows sell out fast. TPG runs late buses to most venues.

unique_experiences.activity: Soldes Shopping on Rue du Rhône
unique_experiences.where: Rue du Rhône, Rue du Marché, Globus, Bongénie
unique_experiences.description: Take advantage of mid-month markdowns in Geneva's luxury and mid-market boutiques — watches, leather, and Swiss-made knitwear.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Weekdays are quieter and staff have more time to help. The covered Rues Basses provide shelter from rain or bise.

reasons_to_visit.details: Lake Geneva at 22–23°C is warm enough for proper swimming. Bains des Pâquis, Plage des Eaux-Vives, and Genève-Plage hum from morning to midnight.
Musiques en été, OpenAir Cinéma, and a packed Fête de la Musique afterglow mean free concerts and films almost every evening.

reasons_to_visit.reason: Long Hot Evenings on the Quais
reasons_to_visit.details: Sunsets near 21:30, lit-up Jet d'Eau against deep blue skies, and apéros on every quai stretch nightly social life into the small hours.
All Salève, Jura, Mont Blanc–area, and Vaud Alps trails are accessible. Day trips to Chamonix, Verbier, or Les Diablerets are easy from Geneva.
End of July sees Fêtes de Genève sound-checks and the city preparing for Switzerland's August 1 National Day fireworks and parades.
Conference and UN agency travel quiets in late July, freeing up better mid-week hotel deals than June or September.

reasons_to_reconsider.details: Heatwaves push afternoon temperatures past 32°C. Many Geneva hotels (especially Old Town and Pâquis townhouses) lack full AC; check before booking in heat-wave forecasts.
Reformation Wall, Bourg-de-Four, and Jet d'Eau jetty all see significant midday crowds, and the Mouettes water buses queue at peak times.
Heatwaves often end in dramatic Alpine thunderstorms — occasionally cancelling lake activities or open-air concerts.
Geneva's beloved baths are wonderful but extremely busy on hot weekends — pre-9am or post-19:00 best.
Quai-side terraces ratchet up their summer tariffs; plan apéros at neighborhood bistros for value.
Grass pollen remains active early July; UV intense throughout.

weather.precipitation: Hot and largely dry, with occasional thunderstorms. Lake at 22°C and warming.
weather.general_tips: Sun protection, hydration, and a hotel with AC for heatwaves are essential.

tourism_level.crowds: Busy throughout. Weekends and bank holidays especially so.
tourism_level.pricing: Peak summer rates; lakefront properties at year-high.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Mediterranean — Geneva tilts toward outdoor living, with the lake as the city's daytime and nighttime square.

events_holidays.name: Montreux Jazz Festival Begins
events_holidays.description: World-famous festival up the lake — many travellers base in Geneva and take train day trips.
events_holidays.notes: Train Geneva → Montreux runs 70 minutes; late return trains laid on during festival.

events_holidays.name: Paléo Festival (Nyon)
events_holidays.description: One of Europe's largest open-air music festivals, 25 minutes from Geneva by train.
events_holidays.notes: Day tickets sell out; many visitors stay in Geneva and commute.

events_holidays.name: OpenAir Cinema Continues
events_holidays.description: Free outdoor cinema along the lakefront.
events_holidays.notes: Bring layers; lake breeze cool after dark.

unique_experiences.description: Swim at 6:30 am as the city wakes — Mont Blanc lit pink, joggers along the quais, the lake at its calmest.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Bring towel and locker coin. Coffee and tartines at the buvette afterwards.

unique_experiences.description: Jazz, soul, rock, and electronic legends on multiple lakefront stages — many free fringe events too.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Combine paid main-stage ticket with free Music in the Park stages. Last train back leaves around 01:30 during festival.

unique_experiences.description: Geneva's flagship public beach: sandy shore, lawns, lake swimming, restaurant kiosks, and open-air cinema at night.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Free entry. Arrive by 10 to claim shaded spots.

unique_experiences.activity: Vineyard Hike to Russin & Cellar Lunch
unique_experiences.where: Geneva canton, west of the city
unique_experiences.description: Take the train to Russin, walk the rolling Mandement vineyards above the Rhône gorge, and lunch at a family vineyard restaurant.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Train 12 minutes from Cornavin. Book lunch in advance at vineyards like Domaine des Balisiers.

weather.precipitation: Hot, often with intense afternoon storms during heatwave breaks.
weather.general_tips: Plan morning activity windows in hot spells; evenings ideal for everything else.

tourism_level.crowds: Sustained peak. Fêtes de Genève sound-checks attract early festival traffic at end of month.
tourism_level.pricing: Peak. Some softening late month as business travel falls away.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Pre-festival anticipation building toward Fêtes de Genève. The city's outdoor calendar is at full throttle.

events_holidays.name: Fêtes de Genève Build-Up
events_holidays.description: Stages, sound systems, and food markets installed along the lakefront for the city's main summer festival.
events_holidays.notes: Some quai-side traffic disruption; lake walks unaffected.

events_holidays.name: Paléo Festival Closing
events_holidays.description: Final big nights of Paléo, 25 minutes from Geneva by train.
events_holidays.notes: Buy single-day tickets in advance. Last trains laid on for festival-goers.

events_holidays.name: Bastille Day Spillover
events_holidays.description: French national day fireworks in nearby French border towns (Annemasse, Évian, Annecy). Cross-border visitors arrive in Geneva.
events_holidays.notes: Trains and buses to French border towns busy in the evening.

unique_experiences.activity: Sunset SUP from Bains des Pâquis
unique_experiences.description: Paddleboard north toward the Jet d'Eau just as the lights come on and Mont Blanc fades to pink.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Rentals available right at the baths. Stay close to shore; storms can build fast.

unique_experiences.where: CGN evening cruise from Quai du Mont-Blanc
unique_experiences.description: Long July dusks make a Friday or Saturday-evening dinner cruise particularly atmospheric.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Book dining slot in advance. Light evening jacket for top deck.

unique_experiences.description: Catch first cable car up at Chamonix and stand at 3,842m as light hits Mont Blanc.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Book first slot online. Start from Geneva at 5 am; allow full day.

unique_experiences.where: Cour de l'Hôtel de Ville, Parc La Grange
unique_experiences.description: Late-month closing nights bring some of the festival's best ensembles.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Arrive 30 minutes early.

reasons_to_visit.details: Europe's largest closed-water regatta sets out from Geneva in mid-June, with up to 500 sailboats racing to the eastern end of Lake Geneva and back — spectacular from the quais and Pâquis.

reasons_to_visit.reason: Open-Air Cinema, Concerts & Lake Festivals Begin
reasons_to_visit.details: Musiques en été, OpenAir Cinéma at Plage des Eaux-Vives, and the first major outdoor concert programmes launch, making warm evenings unforgettable.
Sunset stretches to 21:30 around the solstice. Endless evenings on the quais, late suppers, and post-dinner swims at Bains des Pâquis.
Lake temperatures climb into the high teens by month-end. Bains des Pâquis, Plage des Eaux-Vives, and the new Plage Genève draw locals and visitors alike.
Higher trails (Salève, Jura, lower Mont Blanc area) are accessible from early June. Wildflowers explode across the foothills.
Pre-Fêtes de Genève and pre-summer-holiday timing means slightly softer rates than mid-July onwards.

reasons_to_reconsider.details: June is the year's storm peak — short, sometimes violent thunderstorms can cancel boats and outdoor concerts at short notice.
UN sessions, watch industry visitors, and trade events keep weekday rates high in Pâquis and around Place des Nations.
European river-cruise pre/post packages route through Geneva. Reformation Wall and Bourg-de-Four can see brief midday crowds.
Jet d'Eau jetty, Mouettes ferry stops, and Cathédrale Saint-Pierre towers are at their busiest in June afternoons.
Lakefront terraces dial up summer pricing; reserve and budget accordingly.
European school groups visit museums (CERN, ICRC, MAH) in considerable numbers — late afternoons are calmer.

weather.precipitation: Warm with regular afternoon and evening thunderstorms. Lake at 18–20°C and warming.
weather.general_tips: Carry water and a compact umbrella. Plan outdoor cultural events with a Plan B.

tourism_level.crowds: Building rapidly. Strong weekend volume; weekdays held up by business travel.
tourism_level.pricing: Rising; lakefront properties near peak. Book early.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Effervescent — Genevans rediscover the lake, terraces overflow, and the first big festival energy arrives.

events_holidays.name: Bol d'Or Mirabaud Regatta
events_holidays.description: Iconic two-day regatta starting in Geneva, with hundreds of sailboats racing the length of the lake. Spectacular start line off the Geneva quais.
events_holidays.notes: Best viewing: Quai Gustave-Ador, Bains des Pâquis pier, paddle-steamer cruise to follow the fleet.

events_holidays.name: Geneva Pride (when in June)
events_holidays.date: Some years late May or June
events_holidays.description: Pride parade through central Geneva with concerts and stages at Plainpalais.
events_holidays.notes: Tram diversions; festive atmosphere across the city.

events_holidays.name: Musiques en été Opens
events_holidays.description: Free open-air classical, jazz, and world-music concerts in courtyards and parks, running through August.
events_holidays.notes: Programme on geneve.ch — arrive early for popular venues.

unique_experiences.where: Quai Gustave-Ador, Bains des Pâquis, or paddle-steamer 'race-follower' cruise
unique_experiences.description: Watch the spectacular mass start from the Geneva quais, then either picnic and follow on shore or take a dedicated CGN spectator cruise that shadows the fleet down the lake.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Book spectator cruises weeks in advance. Bring sunscreen, hat, water.

unique_experiences.description: Geneva's iconic small yellow water buses cross the lake every ten minutes — a cheap, charming way to circle the city by water.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Covered by Geneva Transport Card. Easy hop-on/hop-off with city walks.

unique_experiences.where: Cour de l'Hôtel de Ville, Parc La Grange, Place des Volontaires
unique_experiences.description: Free open-air classical, jazz, and world music in some of Geneva's most atmospheric outdoor venues — bring a blanket.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Arrive 30 minutes early. Free programme available at Tourism Office.

unique_experiences.activity: Jet d'Eau Jetty Walk at Sunset
unique_experiences.description: Walk to the end of the jetty for a soaking-distance encounter with Geneva's iconic 140m water plume against pink solstice skies.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Bring a towel — spray drifts in the wind. Beautiful for photography.

weather.precipitation: Hot spells alternate with thunderstorms. Lake at 20–21°C — comfortable swimming.
weather.general_tips: Hydrate frequently; UV is intense around the solstice. Plan late-evening walks for the cool.

tourism_level.crowds: Busy. Family travel begins late June; restaurant tables tighten.
tourism_level.pricing: Near-peak; weekend rates the year's highest before the August dip in business travel.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Long days, packed terraces, lake swimming, open-air concerts. Geneva at its most festive.

events_holidays.name: Summer Solstice / Fête de la Musique
events_holidays.description: Free city-wide music festival with hundreds of concerts in parks, squares, courtyards, and the Old Town.
events_holidays.notes: Programme published a week in advance — pick favourites and plan a walking route.

events_holidays.name: OpenAir Cinema Begins
events_holidays.description: Free open-air cinema at Plage des Eaux-Vives and other venues, including arthouse and family programming.
events_holidays.notes: Bring a blanket. Sound infrastructure ensures decent audio outdoors.

events_holidays.name: Geneva Lake Festival Build-Up
events_holidays.description: Sound systems and stages installed along the quais ahead of the major summer festivals.
events_holidays.notes: Some quai-side traffic disruptions; lake walks unaffected.

unique_experiences.activity: Fête de la Musique Walking Tour
unique_experiences.where: Old Town, Place du Bourg-de-Four, Parc des Bastions, Plainpalais
unique_experiences.description: Wander a self-curated path through Geneva's free Solstice music festival — every courtyard a new band, every park a new stage.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Comfortable shoes; carry water; programme app extremely useful.

unique_experiences.activity: OpenAir Cinema at Plage des Eaux-Vives
unique_experiences.description: Watch a film outdoors on Geneva's new public beach, with the lake breeze and the city skyline.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Free; arrive 90 minutes early for blanket-spot. Snacks and drinks for sale.

unique_experiences.description: Rent SUPs from the baths' shore concession and paddle out toward the Jet d'Eau for the iconic lake-level view.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Morning lake is calmest. Bring waterproof phone pouch.

unique_experiences.where: Wine bars in Eaux-Vives or Carouge (e.g., Le Verre à Monique, La Cave de la Vigneronne)
unique_experiences.description: Solstice-weekend wine bars run flights of Genevan Gamay, Gamaret, and Chasselas, often paired with cheese boards.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Reserve weekend slots.

reasons_to_visit.details: By mid-March, Parc des Bastions, the Botanical Gardens, and the Treille promenade are flecked with crocuses and the first cherry blossoms. Days are noticeably longer and Mont Blanc views, when clear, are stunning.

reasons_to_visit.reason: Salon de l'Auto / Motor-Show Replacement Events
reasons_to_visit.details: Although the historic Geneva International Motor Show has paused, March still brings replacement automotive, design, and trade events at Palexpo, with hotel demand spiking on those weekends.
Sunny days, soft afternoon snow, and longer light make March one of the best months for skiing in Chamonix, Verbier, and the Portes du Soleil — from Geneva you can be on the lifts within two hours.

reasons_to_visit.reason: Easter & Early School Holidays (Late March in Some Years)
reasons_to_visit.details: When Easter falls late March, family-friendly chocolatiers around Rue du Marché and Carouge fill with elaborate Easter eggs and tasting events.
Bains des Pâquis daytime crowds return on sunny weekends, the first lake-shore runners are out by 7am, and Quai du Mont-Blanc terraces start setting tables back outside.
Outside Palexpo trade-fair weekends, hotel rates are softer than in summer and autumn, with strong availability at lakefront properties.

reasons_to_reconsider.details: March in Geneva can swing from 18°C and sunny to snow flurries and cold rain in 48 hours. Pack for both.
When a major trade show is at Palexpo (near the airport), hotel rates can triple and restaurant tables disappear with no warning.
CGN's full summer schedule (with belle-époque paddle steamers and lake tours) typically starts only in April. March options are short shuttle routes.
Higher Jura and Salève hiking trails can be muddy, icy, or snowbound until end-of-month. Stick to lower lakefront and vineyard walks.
Early tree pollen (hazel, alder) is active by mid-March, which can affect sensitive travellers.
Clocks jump forward in late March, briefly shortening morning light and disrupting cross-border train schedules.

weather.precipitation: Mix of cold spells and milder Atlantic systems. Frost mornings still possible; first 15°C+ afternoons appear on sunny days.
weather.general_tips: Layered clothing with windproof shell. Carry a light scarf for evenings.

tourism_level.crowds: Quiet on non-trade-fair weekdays; sharp spikes when Palexpo events run.
tourism_level.pricing: Mostly low except around Palexpo events. Book lakefront hotels early when trade-show dates are known.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Late winter shading into early spring. The first warm sunny afternoons feel transformative.

events_holidays.name: Palexpo Trade Shows
events_holidays.description: Various major fairs (replacement for Motor Show, watch industry meetings, design events) drive significant visitor traffic.
events_holidays.notes: Hotels near the airport and Place des Nations fill first. Cornavin trains stay calm.

events_holidays.name: Internationales Day of the Francophonie
events_holidays.description: Geneva, as a UN city and home to OIF cultural events, hosts concerts, literary nights, and cinema programming celebrating Francophone culture.
events_holidays.notes: Many events are free and held in libraries, cafés, and the Maison Internationale.

unique_experiences.activity: Parc des Bastions & Reformation Wall Walk
unique_experiences.where: Place de Neuve to Promenade des Bastions
unique_experiences.description: Catch the first spring colour in the historic park beneath the Old Town, with the giant outdoor chess sets back in play and the Reformation Wall's five-metre figures of Calvin, Farel, Bèze, and Knox staring out from the rampart.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Combine with a coffee on Place de Neuve and a wander up Rue de la Cité.

unique_experiences.where: Verbier — train to Le Châble, then bus/cable car
unique_experiences.description: Verbier's altitude (1,500–3,300m) keeps snow superb in March, and longer days make for relaxed sunny lunches on mountain terraces.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Train Geneva → Martigny → Le Châble, then cable car. Allow 2.5 hours each way; many start with a 6am train.

unique_experiences.where: Patek Philippe Museum + Plainpalais watch-industry walk
unique_experiences.description: Combine the museum visit with a walk past historic watch-industry buildings on Rue de Carouge and Boulevard de Saint-Georges.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Patek closed Sun/Mon. Several boutiques offer brief atelier visits by appointment.

unique_experiences.where: Quai du Général-Guisan, Bains des Pâquis, Quai du Mont-Blanc
unique_experiences.description: The Jet d'Eau typically resumes daily operations through March (weather permitting). Loop the lake mouth via the bridges for a 5–7km run with classic views.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Café stops at Pâquis and Eaux-Vives. Hat and gloves still wise early mornings.

weather.precipitation: Warmer and longer days. Occasional sharp cold snaps possible but proper winter days become rare. Atlantic rain bands occasionally pass through.
weather.general_tips: First terrace season begins on sunny weekends. Daylight saving begins in late March — pleasant evenings extend to 20:00.

tourism_level.crowds: Building gently — weekenders from Zurich, Lyon, and Paris start arriving for lake views.
tourism_level.pricing: Soft mid-week, climbing on weekends.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Energetic transition into spring; terraces fill, lake runners multiply, Mont Blanc reasserts itself on clear days.

events_holidays.name: Daylight Saving Time Begins
events_holidays.description: Clocks move forward one hour, giving Geneva long bright evenings.
events_holidays.notes: Cross-border train schedules briefly disrupted; double-check connections to France.

events_holidays.name: Easter (when falling in March)
events_holidays.description: Public holidays Good Friday and Easter Monday — many shops closed, but restaurants and museums open and chocolatiers in full display.
events_holidays.notes: Chocolatiers Auer, du Rhône, and Stettler unveil their Easter collections.

events_holidays.name: Spring Programme at Bâtiment des Forces Motrices
events_holidays.description: The Grand Théâtre de Genève and BFM often programme major opera and orchestral performances heading into spring.
events_holidays.notes: Book ahead; reduced-price last-minute tickets sometimes released.

unique_experiences.where: Pier at Quai du Mont-Blanc → Yvoire (France)
unique_experiences.description: As CGN's spring schedule expands, hop a ferry across to medieval Yvoire on the French shore, with cobbled lanes, flower-draped walls, and lake-view restaurants opening for the season.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Bring passport. Return crossings reduced compared to summer — check timetable.

unique_experiences.description: Take the 50-minute train to Vevey or Lutry and walk a flat-to-gentle section of the Lavaux UNESCO vineyards, with terraces newly pruned and the lake glittering below.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Some cellar doors begin opening for weekend tastings late March. Trains every 30 minutes from Cornavin.

unique_experiences.description: With longer evenings, ride up at 18:00 and walk the plateau for sunset views across the city, lake, and Mont Blanc lit pink.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Last cable car down typically 22:00 in spring. Bring layers — temperatures drop fast.

unique_experiences.where: Auer (Rue de Rive), Du Rhône (Rue de la Confédération), Stettler (Rive)
unique_experiences.description: Geneva's chocolatiers compete on Easter window displays — towering chocolate eggs, hand-painted figurines, and pralinés in spring flavours.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Buy early in the week before Easter to avoid Saturday queues.

reasons_to_visit.reason: Best All-Round Weather of the Year
reasons_to_visit.details: Long daylight hours, mild temperatures in the high teens to mid-20s, and the lake regaining its summer blue without yet drawing peak crowds make May arguably Geneva's best month.

One weekend in May, the Geneva canton's wine estates open their doors for free tastings, with shuttle buses running between vineyards in Satigny, Dardagny, and Russin — a perfect introduction to the city's unsung wine country.
The UNESCO terraced vineyards above Lake Geneva are emerald green in May, and the long-distance footpaths from Lutry to Saint-Saphorin are at their most photogenic.

reasons_to_visit.reason: Lake Cruises & Bains des Pâquis Daytime
reasons_to_visit.details: Boat schedules are at full spring expansion, and Bains des Pâquis reopens for swimming, paddleboarding, and sunset apéros.
Lower Jura and Salève hiking is back at full quality by mid-May. Some high passes still closed, but everything below 1,800m is hikeable.
OSR season finale, Bâtiment des Forces Motrices opera, and the first outdoor concerts on Plainpalais all converge.

reasons_to_reconsider.details: Ascension Thursday and Pentecost weekend trigger four-day breaks and sharp hotel-rate spikes; lakefront properties often sell out.
May brings the year's first big convective storms — sudden, occasionally dramatic, with hail in extreme cases.
Major UN, WHO, and WTO events in May lift weekday hotel demand near Place des Nations.
French rail and Swiss transport disputes occasionally flare around May Day.
Lake-region coach tours pick up rapidly; the Reformation Wall and Place du Bourg-de-Four can have brief midday surges.
Grass-pollen season kicks in mid-month, which can affect sensitive travellers.

weather.precipitation: Generally sunny, with the first proper thunderstorm risk. Lake at 14–15°C, swimming for the brave.
weather.general_tips: Bring layers — mornings still cool — and sunscreen. Carry a packable rain layer for storms.

tourism_level.crowds: Moderate, with Ascension Thursday and the following long weekend drawing strong French and German visitors.
tourism_level.pricing: Mid-range, climbing around bank-holiday windows.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Optimistic, full-spring Geneva: cafés overflowing onto the quais, lakefront runners, locals eating ice cream on the bridges.

events_holidays.name: Fête du Travail (Labour Day)
events_holidays.description: Public holiday across Switzerland — most shops closed, traditional union parade through central Geneva.
events_holidays.notes: Museums and lake cruises operate; restaurants generally open.

events_holidays.name: Ascension Thursday & Bank-Holiday Bridge
events_holidays.description: Public holiday with widespread shop closures, but a busy weekend for tourism. Geneva fills with regional French and German visitors.
events_holidays.notes: Book Friday and Saturday dinners well in advance.

events_holidays.name: Geneva Museum Night (when in May)
events_holidays.description: Many Geneva museums open late, often free, with concerts and special programmes.
events_holidays.notes: Map released in spring; popular venues queue at the door.

unique_experiences.description: Geneva's canton-wide open-cellars weekend: free tastings of Chasselas, Gamay, Gamaret, and Pinot Noir at over 60 family estates, with shuttle buses linking them.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Download the official map. Eat between tastings. CFF/SBB runs extra trains to Satigny and Russin.

unique_experiences.description: Classic 11-km walk along stone-walled vineyard terraces with the lake and Mont Blanc as constant company. Several cellar doors open for lake-view tastings.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Trains from Cornavin to Lutry every 30 minutes; finish at Saint-Saphorin and train back from nearby Rivaz.

unique_experiences.activity: Old Town & Reformation Wall Guided Walk
unique_experiences.where: Place de Neuve → Parc des Bastions → Old Town
unique_experiences.description: Geneva Tourism's free 'Bastions and Old Town' walking tour brings the Reformation, the Republic, and 16th-century printing-press story to life.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Book online at geneve.com. English and French tours run several times a week in spring.

unique_experiences.description: After 18:00, the lake baths' simple buvette serves apéros, tartines, and bowl meals at communal tables, with swimmers and sunset over Mont Blanc.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Cash and Swiss cards only. No reservations.

weather.precipitation: Warmer with rising thunderstorm potential. Lake warming to 17–18°C.
weather.general_tips: Carry water and sunscreen. Plan storm-vulnerable outdoor activities for mornings.

tourism_level.crowds: Busy weekends; cruise ship and coach tour day visits returning in volume to Old Town.
tourism_level.pricing: Climbing toward summer rates. Last good window before June price jumps.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Full-throttle pre-summer: lakefront terraces packed at sunset, runners on quais, picnics at Parc La Grange.

events_holidays.name: Pentecost Weekend (Pentecôte)
events_holidays.description: Whit Monday is a public holiday in many cantons; while Geneva does not officially observe it, regional visitors flood the city.
events_holidays.notes: Lake cruises full; book in advance.

events_holidays.name: OSR Season Finale
events_holidays.description: Orchestre de la Suisse Romande's big closing concerts at Victoria Hall.
events_holidays.notes: Often programmes major Romantic symphonies; book early.

events_holidays.name: Geneva Lake Parade Build-Up
events_holidays.description: Soundsystem trial runs and pop-up events ahead of the major summer lake festivals.
events_holidays.notes: Follow Geneva Tourism feeds for pop-up announcements.

unique_experiences.where: Quai du Mont-Blanc → Yvoire and back
unique_experiences.description: Take a CGN belle-époque paddle steamer cruise with a sit-down lunch in the panoramic dining saloon — peak May light on the lake.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Book lunch slot in advance. Swiss Travel Pass covers regular fare; lunch is supplement.

unique_experiences.activity: Mont Salève via the New Cable Car
unique_experiences.description: The recently renovated téléphérique whisks you to 1,100m. Walk the panoramic ridge, paraglide tandem, or lunch at the plateau restaurant.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Bring passport. Tickets cheaper booked online. Bus 8 to terminus is the simplest approach.

unique_experiences.description: Geneva's giant flea market — antique watches, Swiss design, vintage clothing — followed by an apéro at one of the surrounding student bars.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Negotiate gently; bring cash. Tram 12 or 15.

unique_experiences.description: Cycle the rolling vineyards west of the city via signposted route 46, stopping at family wineries and the Rhône gorge viewpoints.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Genève Roule e-bikes recommended. Allow half a day; train back from Russin.

monthly.monthly-taglines: Geneva is at its best in late spring (May–June) and early autumn (September), when the lake warms, Mont Blanc stays clear, and café terraces along the quais run late. July and August bring fireworks, Musiques en été concerts, and lake swimming—balanced against peak hotel rates and August 1 closures. Winter (December–February) is fondue, Escalade festival, and ski-resort access from Cornavin; March eases the city back into the open with longer light and softer prices.

reasons_to_visit.details: One of Europe's largest urban running events takes over the Old Town on the first weekend of December but November weekends are full of preparation runs, costume parades, and family festival energy.
The city's light-art festival illuminates monuments, parks, and public buildings with site-specific installations through late November and December — a magical reason to visit in low light.
Game menus continue at their richest. November is peak season for partridge, hare, and the late-autumn root vegetables.
Geneva's main Christmas market at Jardin Anglais opens in mid-to-late November, with chalets, vin chaud, raclette, and crafts running through December.
Between conferences and pre-Christmas tourism, mid-November is one of the year's best urban-luxury value windows.
Glacier resorts like Zermatt and Saas-Fee open early; cross-country trails appear in the Jura. Day trips begin to work for early-season turns.

reasons_to_reconsider.details: Geneva basin frequently sits under cold low cloud for days, while the Jura and Salève bask in sunshine above the inversion layer.
Sunset by 17:00 limits sightseeing windows. Lighting at the Lux Festival becomes one of the principal evening draws.
Lakefront kiosks shut; Bains des Pâquis switches firmly to winter sauna mode.
Cold northeasterly winds make outdoor walking feel rawer than the thermometer suggests.
From mid-November, the city centre and Rues Basses fill with Christmas shoppers — particularly weekends.
CGN runs only minimal winter service.

weather.precipitation: Cool and often grey. Rain and fog dominate; some clear bise days.
weather.general_tips: Pack waterproof shell, warm layers, and grippy shoes for damp cobblestones.

tourism_level.crowds: Light. Conferences drive some weekday demand.
tourism_level.pricing: Soft mid-week, modest weekend uplift.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Quietly working late autumn. Fondue, fog, and warm light from Old Town windows.

events_holidays.name: All Saints' Day (Toussaint)
events_holidays.description: Catholic observance — not a Geneva public holiday but observed in border France with full closures.
events_holidays.notes: Cross-border day trips affected. No impact within the city.

events_holidays.name: Lux Festival Opens (variable)
events_holidays.description: Light installations across the city — monuments, parks, public buildings.
events_holidays.notes: Free; programme released two weeks ahead. Plan an evening walking route.

events_holidays.name: Chasse Season Continues
events_holidays.description: Traditional restaurants run game menus.
events_holidays.notes: Book a week ahead for weekends.

unique_experiences.description: Indoor day with the world's premier watch museum — three hours pass effortlessly.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Closed Sun/Mon.

unique_experiences.description: When Geneva sits under fog, the Salève plateau often basks in winter sunshine.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Bring passport, sunglasses, warm layers.

unique_experiences.where: Les Armures, Café du Bourg-de-Four, Café Papon
unique_experiences.description: Combine a walk past the cathedral and Treille promenade with a cosy fondue in a 17th-century cave.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Reserve weekends.

unique_experiences.description: Perfect indoor day for the most thought-provoking museum in Geneva.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Closed Mondays. Allow 2 hours.

weather.precipitation: Cold, frequent fog and overcast. Snow possible at city level late month; reliable above 1,000m.
weather.general_tips: Full winter gear. Plan around indoor and illuminated outdoor experiences.

tourism_level.crowds: Christmas market and pre-Escalade build-up bring weekend energy to the Old Town.
tourism_level.pricing: Climbing slightly as Christmas tourism begins.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Pre-festive. Decorations going up; mulled wine appearing on terraces; Lux Festival lighting the city after dark.

events_holidays.name: Geneva Christmas Markets Open
events_holidays.description: Main market at Jardin Anglais; smaller markets at Fusterie and Noël au Quai.
events_holidays.notes: Bring cash and warm layers. Weekend evenings busiest.

events_holidays.name: Lux Festival in Full Swing
events_holidays.description: Site-specific light installations across the city — Old Town, Plainpalais, Pâquis.
events_holidays.notes: Free. Plan a self-guided evening walking route.

events_holidays.name: Course de l'Escalade Preparation
events_holidays.description: Training runs and costume rehearsals visible across the Old Town in the lead-up to the December race.
events_holidays.notes: Festive atmosphere in the cobbled streets.

unique_experiences.description: Geneva's main Christmas market: chalets, vin chaud, raclette, crafts, and a Ferris wheel with lake views.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Cash useful. Evening fairy lights are magical.

unique_experiences.where: Old Town to Pâquis (route varies year to year)
unique_experiences.description: Walk a self-curated route through 10–15 light installations: projection-mapped façades, illuminated trees, interactive sculptures.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Free. Bring warm layers; allow 2 hours.

unique_experiences.activity: Bains des Pâquis Winter Sauna + Soup
unique_experiences.description: Winter sauna with a lake view, then a bowl of the famous soup at the buvette.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Bring towel, swimwear, sandals. Sauna schedule extends into the evening.

unique_experiences.description: Independent watchmakers, jewellers, milliners, and chocolatiers prepare festive collections.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Tram 12. Many ateliers Wed–Sat only.

reasons_to_visit.reason: Autumn Colour Along the Lake & in the Jura
reasons_to_visit.details: Lakefront plane trees, parks, and the Jura forests light up in golds and reds — particularly stunning on Salève cable-car rides and Lavaux vineyard walks.

reasons_to_visit.reason: Nuit des Bains (Quartier des Bains Open Night)
reasons_to_visit.details: Three times a year (typically May, September, and November) but October programmes still energise Geneva's contemporary art quarter with openings, performances, and crowded after-parties.
October is peak hunting season — venison, wild boar, chamois, and pheasant fill menus at traditional bistros and Michelin-starred restaurants alike.
Cool crisp days reward Old Town walking and museum visits, with terraces still open on sunny afternoons.
Outside major conferences, hotel rates ease meaningfully from September highs — a good urban-luxury value window.
Opera, orchestra, dance, and museum programmes are fully back. Grand Théâtre, BFM, and Victoria Hall all run flagship productions.

reasons_to_reconsider.details: Geneva basin inversions become common mid-October, sometimes shrouding the city for days while sunshine persists above 700m.
Sunset moves earlier through the month, with daylight saving ending late October cutting the evening light meaningfully.
Genève-Plage and Plage des Eaux-Vives close. Bains des Pâquis swimming for the very hardy.
Snow can arrive at 1,500m by month-end; higher trails close or require winter equipment.
Chasse season fills traditional restaurants quickly; book a week or more in advance for weekend dinners.
Time change on the last weekend creates transient train and meeting disruption.

weather.precipitation: Variable: warm sunny afternoons alongside cooler rainy fronts. Some lingering bise.
weather.general_tips: Layered clothing; light waterproof. Sunglasses still useful in clear afternoons.

tourism_level.crowds: Moderate; conferences and watch industry keep weekday hotels busy.
tourism_level.pricing: Mid-range, climbing with major business events.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Energetic urban autumn — café season, terraces still in use, lakefront runners and walkers.

events_holidays.name: Marathon de Genève (variable)
events_holidays.description: City marathon and half-marathon — major spectator-friendly event.
events_holidays.notes: Tram detours on race day; check timing if needing the lakefront.

events_holidays.name: BFM Opera & Concert Premieres
events_holidays.description: Major opera and concert premieres at Bâtiment des Forces Motrices.
events_holidays.notes: Book in advance for opening nights.

events_holidays.name: Watch Industry Events
events_holidays.description: Industry showcases drive weekday hotel demand.
events_holidays.notes: Lakefront and Pâquis hotels firm up early.

unique_experiences.where: Café du Soleil, Brasserie Lipp, Les Armures (Old Town)
unique_experiences.description: Traditional Geneva game season — venison medallions with red cabbage and spätzli, wild boar terrines, and pheasant ragouts on every classic menu.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Reserve a week ahead for weekends. Pair with local Gamaret.

unique_experiences.description: Golden-orange beech and oak forests, big views, and crisp air. Lunch at La Corraterie or pack a picnic.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Bring passport. Bus 8 to terminus. Last cable car down earlier in autumn — check.

unique_experiences.where: Espace Saint-Pierre & Musée International de la Réforme
unique_experiences.description: Combine cathedral, Site Archéologique, and the Reformation Museum at Maison Mallet for a deep dive into Geneva's Calvinist history.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Allow 3 hours combined. Closed Mondays.

unique_experiences.description: Student-and-creatives-energised neighbourhood with strong wine bars, natural-wine spots, and great cheese boards.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Tram 12 or 15. Many bars run all-night Thursdays.

weather.precipitation: Cooler. Frequent fog. Snowline drops in surrounding Alps; first frosts possible in the basin.
weather.general_tips: Bring warmer layers, waterproof shell, and grippy shoes for damp cobblestones.

tourism_level.crowds: Quieter on weekends; conferences still drive weekdays.
tourism_level.pricing: Soft on weekends; weekday rates conference-dependent.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Late autumn — fondue starting to reappear, fog rolling off the lake, Old Town lit warmly at dusk.

events_holidays.name: Daylight Saving Ends
events_holidays.description: Clocks fall back; sunsets jump to around 17:30.
events_holidays.notes: Evenings shorten markedly; train and meeting transitions need attention.

events_holidays.name: Nuit des Bains (when in October)
events_holidays.description: Quartier des Bains galleries open late with performances and after-parties.
events_holidays.notes: Map available from galleries; well-attended.

events_holidays.name: Halloween in Bars & Bistros
events_holidays.description: Carouge bars and Pâquis lounges run themed nights — moderate Halloween presence.

unique_experiences.description: Geneva's basin fog makes the perfect day to take three top museums back-to-back.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Closed Mondays. Combine with a fondue dinner.

unique_experiences.description: The terraced vineyards turn a copper-gold over the lake — one of Switzerland's signature autumn walks.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Trains every 30 min. Allow 3–4 hours walking plus tasting stops.

unique_experiences.description: Contemporary art galleries open late, with performances, DJs, and crowded after-parties.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Free entry. Map at any participating gallery.

unique_experiences.description: Sauna and hammam season is fully back. Sweat with a lake view, then plunge into the cool lake.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Bring sandals, towel, and swimwear.

reasons_to_visit.reason: Best Weather Window of the Year
reasons_to_visit.details: Warm sunny days in the low 20s, cool nights, very low rainfall, and clear visibility for Mont Blanc — many Geneva regulars consider September the city's perfect month.
Mandement, Satigny, and Dardagny vineyards are at full harvest, with wine festivals and open cellars across the Geneva canton.
Grand Théâtre, Victoria Hall, BFM, and museums launch new programmes after the summer break — a strong month for opera, classical music, and major exhibitions.
Salève, Jura crest trails, and Mont Blanc–area hikes are dry, cool, and clear. Wildflowers have shifted to autumn yellows and reds.
Returning UN agenda and World Trade Organisation activity make Geneva energetic, but weekend tourism remains comfortable.
Some autumn arts and architectural light installations begin in late September, previewing winter cultural programming.

reasons_to_reconsider.details: From mid-month, UN events and watch industry traffic push weekday hotel rates near year-high, particularly in Pâquis and around Place des Nations.
Mornings drop to 10–12°C; lake-front evenings need a light jacket. Lake swimming still possible but cooler.
Musiques en été and OpenAir Cinema have ended; outdoor concerts limited to occasional festival days.
Some Atlantic fronts arrive late month, ending the dry spell. Pack for variable weather.
Post-summer business travel and conference visitors snap up the best fine-dining tables; book ahead.
Boat schedules contract from late September onwards as the autumn timetable takes over.

weather.precipitation: Mostly dry, with stable high pressure. Lake at 21°C.
weather.general_tips: Light layers; light evening jacket. Sunglasses and sunscreen still essential.

tourism_level.crowds: Moderate. Weekend leisure travel strong; weekdays driven by business and diplomatic visitors.
tourism_level.pricing: Climbing on weekdays; reasonable on weekends.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Glorious early autumn — the Geneva rentrée energy combined with summer-soft evenings.

events_holidays.name: Geneva Wine Harvest Programming
events_holidays.description: Vineyards across Mandement and Satigny host harvest events, sometimes including open-pickings and tasting weekends.
events_holidays.notes: Reserve where required; many events run Friday evening through Sunday.

events_holidays.name: OSR Season Launch
events_holidays.description: Orchestre de la Suisse Romande opens its concert season at Victoria Hall.
events_holidays.notes: Book early for headliner programmes.

events_holidays.name: Geneva International Film Festival Tous Écrans (variable)
events_holidays.description: International film and series festival across cinemas in Geneva.
events_holidays.notes: Check programme; tickets accessible.

unique_experiences.description: Walk between family vineyards harvesting and pressing, with tastings of the previous year's vintage and a chance to see this year's grapes coming in.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Train from Cornavin to Satigny. Some estates require reservation.

unique_experiences.description: Climb the tower for the lake-and-Mont-Blanc panorama, then descend to explore the remarkable 4th-century archaeological site beneath the cathedral.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Combined ticket. Allow 2 hours.

unique_experiences.where: Col de la Faucille, Le Reculet, La Dôle
unique_experiences.description: Bus and cable car into the Jura ridge above Geneva for one of the best Mont Blanc panoramas in Europe.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Allow full day. Bring proper hiking shoes and layers.

unique_experiences.description: Long calm sunsets and Mont Blanc reflections — late summer's perfect lakefront moment.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Free walking; nearby buvettes for drinks.

weather.precipitation: First Atlantic fronts arrive. Generally still pleasant with some rainy stretches.
weather.general_tips: Pack a light rain layer. Cooler nights — long-sleeve essentials.

tourism_level.crowds: Moderate to busy on weekdays driven by business travel; quieter weekends.
tourism_level.pricing: Weekday hotels at year-high or near it; weekends softer.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Energetic, professional Geneva — packed cafés in the morning, lake walks at lunch, concert hall and opera in the evening.

events_holidays.name: Jeûne Genevois (Geneva Fast)
events_holidays.date: Thursday after first Sunday of September
events_holidays.description: Geneva-specific public holiday with widespread closures. Traditional plum tart (tarte aux pruneaux) eaten at home.
events_holidays.notes: Most shops closed; museums open. Plan a quiet day or day-trip.

events_holidays.name: Watches & Wonders Adjacent Events
events_holidays.description: Industry brand pop-ups and showcases continue post-spring fair.
events_holidays.notes: Check Rue du Rhône boutique schedules.

events_holidays.name: Marathon de Genève (variable)
events_holidays.description: Geneva's main marathon — full and half — winds through the city and lakefront.
events_holidays.notes: Spectator-friendly. Tram diversions on race day.

unique_experiences.where: Patisseries across the city (Auer, Aux Trois Brins)
unique_experiences.description: The Jeûne Genevois tradition: a buttery short-pastry plum tart eaten throughout Geneva on the holiday weekend.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Order in advance for whole tarts.

unique_experiences.description: Take the Geneva → Brig → Bellinzona train (4 hours one way) for a long-day excursion to Italian-speaking Switzerland's UNESCO castles.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Long but stunning. Best on a Saturday with clear weather.

unique_experiences.where: Patek Philippe Museum + Rue du Rhône
unique_experiences.description: Combine the museum visit with a curated walk of major Genevan haute-horlogerie boutiques.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Reserve museum visits in advance.

unique_experiences.where: Le Verre à Monique (Carouge), La Cave de la Vigneronne (Eaux-Vives)
unique_experiences.description: Compare Genevan Gamay, Gamaret, and Pinot Noir from the just-pressing 2024 (when available) and recent vintages.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Reserve weekends; cheese boards pair well.
